assertions.title: Key Worker Session Templates and Action Tracking

steps.action: Open the keyworker session form for Mia Dawson.
steps.expected: Structured template includes emotional check-in, week review, care plan goal progress, education, health, family contact, wishes/feelings, and action tasks assignable to staff.
steps.observed: Form included check-in, week review, education, health, family/contact, wishes/feelings, and action description + due date only; no visible goal-progress section or assignee field.

steps.action: Add an action and save the session.
steps.expected: Action is tracked as a task with assignee and due date.
steps.observed: Session saved and showed a pending-action badge, but the action had only description + due date and no staff assignment.

steps.action: Review the manager dashboard after data exists.
steps.expected: Dashboard shows session compliance and overdue actions.
steps.observed: Dashboard remained a placeholder with 'Dashboard widgets will be populated as features are added in upcoming milestones.' and no session-compliance or overdue-action widget.

assertions.issues: The session form is only partially structured, actions cannot be assigned to specific staff, and the dashboard exposes no session-compliance or overdue-action tracking.

assertions.title: Restraint Recording with Debrief Workflow

steps.action: Open the restraint form.
steps.expected: Form captures child/staff involved, antecedent, approved technique, start/end or duration, injury checks, post-incident observations, and sign-off workflow cues.
steps.observed: Form exposed date/time, duration, technique, reason, injury-check checkboxes, and free-text debrief boxes only; there were no child/staff involved fields, antecedent field, start/end times, post-incident observations, or sign-off controls.

steps.action: Save a restraint record without entering debrief details.
steps.expected: Mandatory debrief workflow blocks completion or auto-creates separate debrief tasks for child and staff.
steps.observed: The record saved successfully with blank debriefs, then the keyworker page only showed a warning banner that a restraint record was missing debrief.

steps.action: Look for management review / restraint register surfaces.
steps.expected: A management sign-off interface and restraint register are available.
steps.observed: No management sign-off action or dedicated restraint register surface was exposed in the tested UI.

assertions.issues: Debriefs are not mandatory, no debrief tasks are created, and the tested UI lacks management sign-off and restraint-register views.

assertions.title: Sanctions Log with Prohibited Measures Safeguards

steps.action: Open the sanctions form.
steps.expected: Form records sanction details and blocks prohibited sanctions with a clear 'Prohibited — cannot be used' message.
steps.observed: The form offered a checkbox labelled 'This is a prohibited measure' instead of blocking prohibited sanctions.

steps.action: Attempt to record a prohibited sanction.
steps.expected: Submission is blocked.
steps.observed: The save button changed to 'Record prohibited measure' and the prohibited sanction saved successfully.

steps.action: Review the saved sanction.
steps.expected: Only permitted sanctions appear in the sanctions log / Reg 44 view.
steps.observed: The keyworker page displayed a management-warning banner for the prohibited sanction, proving the UI allows prohibited entries rather than preventing them; no Reg 44 visitor view was discovered during the tested flow.

assertions.issues: Prohibited sanctions are warn-only and still recordable, which does not satisfy the required safeguard.

assertions.title: Visitor Log (Schedule 4 Compliance)

steps.action: Open the visitor log page.
steps.expected: The home-level visitor log is searchable/filterable and offers entry creation.
steps.observed: The page showed counts and an empty-state table, but no visible search, date-range, or filter controls in the tested owner session.

steps.action: Open the new visitor entry surface.
steps.expected: A visitor-entry form captures visitor name, time in/out, reason, and who was visited.
steps.observed: Navigating to /child-core-d-home/visitor-log/new reached /child-core-d-home/permission-denied and rendered a 404 page instead of a usable form.

steps.action: Verify inspector / Reg 44 access and populated logging.
steps.expected: The completed log is accessible to inspectors / Reg 44 visitors.
steps.observed: Because entry creation failed and no Reg 44-specific viewer surface was exposed in the tested flow, inspector access could not be validated.

assertions.issues: The tested owner session could not open the new-visitor page and the visitor-log surface does not expose the required search/filter controls.

assertions.title: Children's Wishes and Feelings Recording

steps.action: Open and complete the wishes-and-feelings form.
steps.expected: Staff can record the child's own words, context, and any action taken.
steps.observed: The form captured date, category, method, the child's words, and action taken, and the entry saved successfully.

steps.action: Look for a care-decision prompt referencing saved wishes/feelings.
steps.expected: Care decision forms prompt staff to reference relevant wishes-and-feelings records.
steps.observed: No care-decision prompt or referencing workflow surfaced anywhere in the tested wishes/keyworker flow.

steps.action: Inspect the child's LAC surface for an auto-generated voice summary.
steps.expected: A child's voice summary is generated for LAC reviews.
steps.observed: The /lac page did not surface any wishes/feelings/voice summary content for the newly recorded entry.

assertions.issues: Basic wishes capture exists, but there is no discovered care-decision linkage prompt and no visible auto-generated LAC review summary.

assertions.title: Children's Meetings Record

steps.action: Inspect the dashboard and person/keyworker surfaces for a meetings entry point.
steps.expected: A house-meetings workflow is discoverable from the children's-home UI.
steps.observed: No meetings navigation or meeting-record entry point was visible on the tested dashboard, person, or keyworker surfaces.

steps.action: Navigate to a likely meetings route.
steps.expected: A meeting template captures attendance, agenda, discussion, decisions, and actions.
steps.observed: GET /child-core-d-home/meetings returned the app's 404 page.

steps.action: Look for action-tracking / Reg 44 viewing surfaces for meetings.
steps.expected: Meeting actions and Reg 44 access are available.
steps.observed: No meeting record surface, action list, or Reg 44 viewing path was discovered in the tested UI.

assertions.issues: No user-facing children's meetings surface was present in the tested application.

assertions.title: Children's Complaints Recording

steps.action: Inspect the children-facing/keyworker surfaces for a complaints entry point.
steps.expected: A dedicated complaint workflow is discoverable.
steps.observed: No complaint form or complaints log entry point was visible in the tested UI.

steps.action: Check nearby surfaces for complaint-related controls.
steps.expected: Complaint raising includes advocacy prompts and staged resolution tracking.
steps.observed: The only complaint-related UI found was 'Complaints' as a category option on the wishes-and-feelings form; there was no dedicated staged complaint workflow or advocacy prompt.

steps.action: Navigate to a likely complaints route.
steps.expected: A complaints page exists.
steps.observed: GET /child-core-d-home/complaints returned the app's 404 page.

assertions.issues: Complaint handling is not implemented as a dedicated user-facing workflow; the tested UI lacks advocacy prompts, staged statuses, satisfaction capture, and report output.

assertions.title: Daily Log / Running Record

steps.action: Open the daily care-note form.
steps.expected: Daily log captures activities, meals, mood, incidents, visitors, contacts, education attendance, bedtime, and handover context.
steps.observed: The care-note form captured note type, shift, mood, personal care, meals/nutrition, mobility, health, handover points, and narrative, but no dedicated fields for visitors, contacts, education attendance, or bedtime.

steps.action: Save a daily note and review the timeline.
steps.expected: Entries are timestamped and attributed to the author.
steps.observed: The note saved successfully and appeared on the care-notes timeline with author and timestamp.

steps.action: Look for an auto-generated handover summary / Reg 44 view.
steps.expected: The system generates handover summary output for the incoming shift and exposes the daily log to Reg 44 viewers.
steps.observed: The UI only provided a manual 'Handover Points' textarea; no generated summary control or Reg 44-specific log view surfaced in the tested flow.

assertions.issues: The care-notes surface is a partial running record only; it lacks several required children-home fields and provides manual handover text instead of an auto-generated handover summary.

frictions.description: Email verification required reading the shared dev-server log, and the first verification redirect left the browser on the verify-email page with stale session state.
frictions.resolution: Recovered the token from the shared dev-server log, hit the verify endpoint, then cleared the isolated browser session cookies/localStorage and logged in again.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 0 passed, 8 failed, 0 blocked. Partial keyworker, wishes, and care-note surfaces exist, but required compliance behaviours are incomplete and the meetings/complaints surfaces are absent.
